prompt: These two threads can deadlock. What is the bug?
choices: Two threads cannot print to System.out simultaneously
T1 acquires lockA then waits for lockB; T2 acquires lockB then waits for lockA — circular dependency causes deadlock
synchronized blocks cannot be nested in Java
lockA and lockB must be declared volatile to be used as monitors
explanation: T1 holds lockA and waits for lockB. T2 holds lockB and waits for lockA. Neither can proceed — deadlock. Fix: always acquire locks in the same order in both threads (both acquire lockA before lockB). This is the lock ordering principle.
